flashcards.front: What is a component in Swing?
flashcards.back: A component is any object that can be displayed on the screen and can interact with the user, such as buttons, labels, and text fields.

flashcards.front: What is a container in Swing?
flashcards.back: A container is a special component that can hold other components and manage their layout, positioning, and sizing.

flashcards.front: Name three top-level containers in Swing.
flashcards.back: JFrame, JDialog, JApplet, and JWindow are all top-level containers.

flashcards.front: What is the content pane in a JFrame?
flashcards.back: The content pane is the main container within a JFrame that holds all the visible components of the window.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between lightweight and heavyweight components?
flashcards.back: Lightweight components (Swing) don't use native peer components, while heavyweight components (AWT) rely on native operating system components.
